basics.name: Lyle Goodyear
basics.label: Predoctoral Research Fellow at Microsoft Research NYC

education.institution: Stanford University, GPA: 3.997, Tau Beta Pi Engineering Honor Society
education.area: Computer Science with Honors (BS), Theory Subplan

work.name: Microsoft Research New York, Economics and Computation Group
work.summary: Advised by Sr. Principal Researcher Markus Mobius.

work.name: Microsoft Research New England, Economics and Computation Group
work.summary: Rational DeGroot Learning: Developed and analyzed extension of DeGroot social learning model. Designed human subject experiment to test theory. Preparing paper for submission to American Economic Review.
highlights: Advised by Sr. Principal Researcher Markus Mobius.

work.name: Stanford Department of Management Science and Engineering
work.summary: State Representation for LLMs in Games: Used LLMs to simulate realistic human economic behavior in multi-agent games. Implemented agent-based network congestion model, designed natural language state representation framework.
highlights: Advised by MS&E Professor Ramesh Johari, mentored by ICME Ph.D. candidate Rachel Guo.
Presented at EC '25 Information Economics X LLMs.

work.name: Stanford Department of Computer Science
work.summary: Benchmark for Autonomous Scientific Discovery Agents: Built benchmark to assess LLMs in model building and experiment design; implemented probabilistic models in Python, built interface between simulated environment and LLM agents.
highlights: Presented at NeurIPS '25 Scaling Environments for Agents.

certificates.name: Retrieving, Processing, and Visualizing Data with Python
